hook.text: Vine invented short-form video. TikTok copied the format and became a $200 billion company. Vine was shut down.

buildup.text: Vine launched in 2013 with six-second looping videos. It became a cultural phenomenon — birthing memes, comedians, and stars.

discovery.text: Twitter acquired Vine pre-launch but never invested — no monetization, no creator fund, no discovery algorithm.

twist.text: Top Vine creators demanded payment. Twitter refused. They migrated to YouTube and Instagram, taking their audiences with them.

climax.text: Twitter shut Vine in 2016. Four years later, TikTok proved the format was worth hundreds of billions. Vine's owners missed it.

punchline.text: The right product at the wrong company still fails.

quiz.question: Why did Vine fail despite inventing short-form video?
options: Twitter underinvested in it — no creator monetization, no algorithm, no funding
The six-second format was too short
